findings.evidence: `rg -n "trivial_.*matrixCoefficientSubmodule.*eq|rankOne.*trivial|span_singleton_one"` finds only `trivial_matrixCoefficient_mem_span_singleton_one` and `trivial_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_le_span_singleton_one`, while the available equality is named only for `groupLike_rankOne_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_eq_span_singleton` at lines 91-96.
findings.fix: Add a trivial-named `@[simp]` theorem for `M := R`, e.g. `trivial_rankOne_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_eq_span_singleton_one : matrixCoefficientSubmodule (R := R) (C := C) (M := R) = Submodule.span R ({1} : Set C)`, proved from the group-like rank-one theorem or directly.
findings.issue: Only `trivial_matrixCoefficientSubmodule_le_span_singleton_one` is exposed for trivial comodules, but the PR target/description needs the rank-one trivial coefficient submodule to be exactly `R • 1`; downstream users should not have to specialize the group-like API to recover the trivial statement.

summary: The trivial-comodule API is missing the advertised rank-one submodule characterization under the trivial instance.
